summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships persistent clipboard-hijacking cryptocurrency clipper
details:
Package name `@jaggle/resizeobserves` is a homoglyph typosquat of the popular `@juggle/resize-observer` (the README even instructs importing from the legitimate package). The package has nothing to do with ResizeObserver: it ships a Python daemon (`clipboard_guardian/guardian.py`) that polls the clipboard every 0.4s, regex-matches cryptocurrency addresses across 35+ chains, and silently rewrites them to attacker-controlled wallets hardcoded in the package (ETH 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18, BTC bc1qs2mpls..., XMR 42zhAidV..., among others), stealing any crypto funds the user attempts to send.

On `npm install`, the postinstall script (`npm-install.js`) executes `pip install --upgrade --break-system-packages` against the bundled Python payload — overriding distro package-management protections — and runs `sudo apt-get install -y xclip` (with pacman/dnf fallbacks) without consent. It then installs persistent autostart under deliberately disguised system-service names: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` on Linux, a LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist` on macOS, and a `PyRuntimeBroker` scheduled task on Windows. At runtime, the daemon further disguises itself by setting its process name to `python3-dbus-helper` / `com.apple.python.runtime` / `Python Runtime Broker` and enumerates monitoring tools (htop, taskmgr, procmon, Activity Monitor, Process Hacker) for evasion.

Multiple independent block signals: typosquat against a high-traffic package + concrete financial-theft payload + install-time privileged system mutation + cross-platform persistence + active runtime concealment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
